item.title: Proclamation Imposing a Temporary Import Surcharge to Address Fundamental International Payments Problems
item.description: A presidential proclamation asserting authority under the Constitution and specified statutes, including section 122 and section 301 of title 3, United States Code, and section 604, to impose a temporary surcharge on imports. The measure is described as aiming to address fundamental international payments problems and outlines the scope and duration of its application. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Continuation of the Suspension of Duty-Free De Minimis Treatment for All Countries
item.description: This presidential action extends the government's policy of suspending duty-free de minimis treatment for imported goods from all countries. It states that the President exercises authority under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974, as amended, to maintain the current import tax treatment for de minimis shipments. The action specifies that the suspension applies to goods from all countries and continues the policy previously in effect. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Executive Action Ending Certain Tariff Actions
item.description: The President utilizes authorities conferred by the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974 to terminate specific tariff actions that were previously in effect. The notice outlines the legal basis, the scope of the tariff actions being ended, and any relevant transition or implementation details associated with the termination. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Executive Order on Ensuring Adequate Domestic Supply of Elemental Phosphorus and Glyphosate-Based Herbicides for National Defense
item.description: This Executive Order, issued under the President's constitutional authority and statutory authorities including the Defense Production Act of 1950, establishes policy and findings related to the defense significance of elemental phosphorus and glyphosate-based herbicides. It aims to ensure an adequate domestic supply of these materials for national defense and critical governmental functions. The order directs relevant federal agencies to assess supply chain vulnerabilities, identify necessary production capacity, and take actions to enhance domestic production and procurement within the scope of the Defense Production Act. It calls for coordination with private sector producers, administration of allocation and prioritization authorities as necessary, and reporting on progress and outcomes to support national security objectives. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Presidential Proclamation Honoring President George Washington on His Birthday and the 250th Anniversary of American Independence, 2026
item.description: A presidential proclamation designates February 22, 2026 as President George Washington's Birthday and commemorates the 250th anniversary of American independence. It recognizes Washington's role in the American Revolution and his contributions to the nation's founding, and it invites appropriate observances, education, and reflection on his legacy and the values underlying the United States' founding. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Executive Action Modifying Tariffs on Imports from the Government of the Russian Federation
item.description: This presidential action, issued under authority from the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974, modifies the duties applicable to imports from the Government of the Russian Federation. The action defines the scope and effective date of tariff changes intended to address threats to the United States posed by the Russian government. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Executive Order Establishing an Arms Transfer Strategy Prioritizing American-Made Military Equipment
item.description: This executive order directs federal agencies to develop and implement a comprehensive arms transfer strategy that prioritizes the export and transfer of American-made military equipment. It calls for leveraging the United States' manufacturing base to maximize defense exports, coordinating interagency efforts across departments, and aligning licensing, policy, and procurement decisions with national security and foreign policy objectives. The order seeks to establish clear objectives, timelines, and governance mechanisms, requires ongoing assessment of market opportunities, and mandates regular reporting to update the strategy to reflect changes in law and international conditions. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.
